top_weaknesses.issue: Speculative figures (median American consuming 400K tokens/day; 3.4% growth in Q1 2027; specific city price drops) presented with too much certainty for an HN audience that pokes at numbers
top_weaknesses.fix: Frame Citrini's numbers as 'in the scenario' projections rather than asserted facts; add one line of caveat after the token-volume claim

top_weaknesses.issue: Analyst-speak phrases break voice: 'underlying physics', 'signature is already in the pipe', 'underweighting', 'downstream balance sheet effects are mechanical'
top_weaknesses.fix: Replace metaphors with concrete mechanism — e.g., one paragraph showing an agent picking FedNow vs Visa with the actual cost delta

top_weaknesses.issue: H2 'The signature is already in the pipe' is metaphor-heavy; informational intent unclear at a glance
top_weaknesses.fix: Rewrite as something like 'Early signals: agents already bypass the checkout moment'

top_weaknesses.issue: Two 80+ word paragraphs (China comparison; networks-fight-back) feel like walls on mobile and bury the payoff
top_weaknesses.fix: Split each at the natural pivot — China paragraph at 'The networks did not get disrupted...'; defense paragraph at 'None of it changes...'

top_weaknesses.issue: Implicit list opportunities buried in prose (rails enumeration, agent decision criteria, defense moves)
top_weaknesses.fix: Bullet the rails (Stablecoins/FedNow/RTP/A2A/closed-loop) with one-line 'why it undercuts interchange' each

consensus_summary: Sharp closing question and a coherent H2 spine that the H2-skimmer rates ready, but the middle drifts into analyst-speak and over-asserts speculative scenario numbers. Tighten claims with caveats, swap one metaphor-heavy H2, and split the two long paragraphs to ship.
